GAMEPLAY_CharcoalPile010Description.English: 10 pieces of charcoal, arranged in a convenient pile. This is too many to draw a map or craft with, but can be unpiled and used one by one.
GAMEPLAY_CharcoalPile010Description.Japanese: 集められた木炭。山になったままでは地図を描けない。

GAMEPLAY_StonePile010Description.English: An orderly pile of 10 small stones. Too many to throw and too few to build with, but can be unpiled and used one by one.

GAMEPLAY_ScrapPile010Description.English: Scrap metal, arranged in a box for easy transport. Too jumbled up to use in their current state, but if you spend a few minutes seperating them, could be used for crafting.

GAMEPLAY_RwoodPile010Description.English: A pile of 10 pieces of reclaimed wood, arranged as neatly as you can manage. Can be burned as is, or unpiled for use in crafting.
GAMEPLAY_RwoodPile010Description.Japanese: 束ねられた回収した木材。

GAMEPLAY_StickPile010Description.English: 10 sticks, bundled together for easy transport. Burns somewhat better than feeding each piece into the fire alone, but not by much.

GAMEPLAY_StickPile050Description.English: A large bundle of sticks, barely able to be fastened together. Burns somewhat more slowly than feeding each piece into a fire alone, but not by very much.

GAMEPLAY_StickPile050FDescription.English: A large bundle of sticks, barely able to be fastened together. Burns somewhat more slowly than feeding each piece into a fire alone, but not by very much.
GAMEPLAY_StickPile050FDescription.Japanese: 並べて束ねられた枝木。

GAMEPLAY_SoftwoodPile005Description.English: A bundle of cedar firewood, cut to length and neatly packed. Can be burned together, or sorted into individual pieces.

GAMEPLAY_HardwoodPile005Description.English: A bundle of good quality hard fir firewood, cut to length and neatly stacked together. Can be burned all at once, or sorted into individual pieces.

GAMEPLAY_CoalPile004Description.English: Four pieces of coal, arranged in a neat stack. Can be burned as is or broken down into individual pieces.

GAMEPLAY_CattailPile005Description.English: Five cat tail stalks, bound together for easy transport. Tough enough to be mistaken for a bundle of branches, but much more nutritious. Can be eaten together as a substantial, if bland, meal, or broken into individual stalks.
